parts.label: PART 3
parts.context: Rachel and Paul discuss studying with the Open University.
parts.transcript_text: Rachel says studying with the Open University required a high level of motivation because students mostly study alone. Combining study with a full-time job made her good at time management. The degree is easier because it is made up of modules, so students can take breaks between them. She also enjoyed summer schools because she met other students there.

groups.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.prompt_html:
1. Studying with the Open University demanded a great deal of .
  2. Studying and working at the same time improved Rachel's skills.
  3. It was helpful that the course was structured in .
  4. She enjoyed meeting other students at .

items.prompt_html: Studying with the Open University demanded a great deal of <blank/>.
Studying and working at the same time improved Rachel's <blank/> skills.
It was helpful that the course was structured in <blank/>.
She enjoyed meeting other students at <blank/>.

q27.explanation: Rachel says she needed to maintain a high level of motivation when studying mostly on her own.
q27.evidence: I found I needed to maintain a high level of motivation.
q27.pitfall: Do not write high level because the sentence already says a great deal of.

q28.explanation: Studying around a full-time job improved her time-management skills.
q28.evidence: I got very good at time-management because I had to fit time for studying round a full-time job.
q28.pitfall: Use no more than two words; skills is already in the prompt.

q29.explanation: Rachel says the degree is made up of modules, which allows breaks between them.
q29.evidence: The degree is made up of modules.
q29.pitfall: Do not write course; the required structure unit is modules.

q30.explanation: Rachel enjoyed meeting other students at summer schools.
q30.evidence: There are summer schools. ... They're great, because you meet all the other people.
q30.pitfall: Tutors are mentioned, but the meeting with other students happens at summer schools.
